description: LogPeck Master Diagnostic Rules - Expert Forensic Edition

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Collection Scan (COLLSCAN)
rules.description: Targeted collection has no index for this query shape. MongoDB must scan every document to satisfy the result set, causing massive CPU and I/O overhead.

rules.condition: SORT in plan_summary and IXSCAN not in plan_summary
rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Unindexed In-Memory Sort
rules.description: Result set was sorted in memory without the help of an index. This is extremely slow for large datasets and carries a high risk of 'Sort exceeded memory limit' errors.

rules.label: ⚠️ [WORKLOAD] High Planning ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: The query optimizer is spending a disproportionate amount of time choosing an execution plan. Often caused by index complexity, high plan cache churn, or lack of hint usage.

rules.label: ⚠️ [WORKLOAD] I/O Bound ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Operation spent over 30% of its time waiting for disk. This indicates heavy physical read/write activity, possible disk throttling, or a 'cold cache' situation.

rules.label: ⚠️ [TRACE] Inefficient Index ({value:.0f}:1)
rules.description: The index used for this query is not selective enough. MongoDB is scanning many index keys to return very few documents, indicating suboptimal index field ordering.

rules.label: 🚨 [WORKLOAD] High Lock Yields ({value:.0f}/op)
rules.description: The operation yielded its lock frequently to other threads. High yield counts often follow high I/O wait times or indicate extreme concurrency stress on the cluster.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Latency Cliff ({value:.1f}x Avg)
rules.description: Extreme performance variance detected. The slowest sample is significantly slower than the average, indicating intermittent blocking, resource spikes, or lock queuing.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Write Conflicts
rules.description: Optimistic concurrency failure. Multiple operations attempted to write to the same document or range simultaneously, forcing the database to retry the operation.

rules.label: 🚨 [WORKLOAD] CPU Bound ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: The operation spent nearly all its time in compute. This is typical for complex aggregations, heavy data encoding/decoding, or cryptographic operations.

rules.label: 📉 [TRACE] Reduction Pipeline ({value:.0f}:1)
rules.description: Stage-heavy aggregation that examines many documents in early stages before collapsing into few results. Often indicates a missing or late $match stage, or a full-collection aggregation (e.g. $group with COLLSCAN).

rules.label: 🐌 [TRACE] Inactive Transaction Wait
rules.description: The multi-statement transaction was open but inactive for more than 50% of its duration. This usually indicates the transaction is waiting on external application logic.

rules.label: 🔗 ACTIVE TRANSACTION
rules.description: Multi-document transaction identified. These operations carry higher overhead and lock pressure than standard CRUD.

rules.label: 🚨 [WORKLOAD] OPLOG WAIT ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Concurrency bottleneck during write operations. Indicates high parallel write pressure or slow oplog application on secondary nodes.

rules.label: 🚨 [WORKLOAD] EXECUTION QUEUE ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Significant ticket queueing detected. The query spent most of its life waiting for an available execution slot, indicating the cluster has reached its concurrency limit.

rules.label: 🔥 [WORKLOAD] LOCK CONTENTION ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: High resource contention. The query spent a significant portion of its life waiting to acquire internal collection or document locks.

rules.label: 🐌 [WORKLOAD] Replication Throttling ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Operation was throttled due to replication lag or secondary sync delays.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] MaxTimeMS Timeout
rules.description: Operation was terminated by the server because it exceeded its 'maxTimeMS' limit. Results are likely incomplete or failed entirely.

rules.label: 🚨 [WORKLOAD] Search Bottleneck ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Atlas Search bottleneck. The database engine spent over 70% of its time waiting for results from the 'mongot' search process.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Regex Scan
rules.description: The query uses a regular expression filter. Regex matches, especially those with the 'i' (case-insensitive) flag or non-anchored patterns, are CPU-intensive and cannot always fully leverage indexes.

rules.label: 🔗 [TRACE] Aggregation Join ($lookup)
rules.description: The query uses a $lookup stage. Joins are non-standard in MongoDB and can cause significant performance degradation if not optimized with indexes on the 'from' collection.

rules.label: 🚨 NOT SUPPORTED ON VIEW
rules.description: Namespace is a view, not a collection. Certain operations (like $collStats or storageStats) cannot be performed directly on views.

rules.label: 🚨 CLIENT DISCONNECT
rules.description: The operation was interrupted because the client closed the connection. Often indicates a client-side timeout or network instability.

rules.label: 🚨 MEMORY LIMIT EXCEEDED
rules.description: The operation exceeded the internal memory limit (typically 100MB for sorts/aggregations). Consider optimizing indexes or enabling 'allowDiskUse'.

rules.label: 🚨 OPERATION INTERRUPTED
rules.description: The operation was interrupted by the server, likely due to a shutdown, stepdown, or manual kill command.

rules.label: 🚨 DUPLICATE KEY
rules.description: Unique constraint violation. The application attempted to insert or update a document with a key that already exists in a unique index.

rules.label: 🚨 UNAUTHORIZED
rules.description: Permission denied. The user authenticated to the session does not have the required RBAC privileges to perform this operation.

rules.label: 🚨 WRITE CONFLICT ERROR
rules.description: Concurrent write operation detected. The storage engine was unable to commit due to a conflict with another transaction. This is a fatal error returned to the application.

rules.label: 🚨 NAMESPACE NOT FOUND
rules.description: The specified database or collection does not exist. Verify the connection string and application logic.

rules.label: 🚨 AUTHENTICATION FAILED
rules.description: Invalid credentials provided for the database user. Check secrets and connection string parameters.

rules.label: 🚨 CURSOR NOT FOUND
rules.description: The cursor requested by getMore has either timed out or was closed on the server. Consider increasing batch sizes or checking cursor lifetimes.

rules.label: 🚨 LOCK TIMEOUT
rules.description: Resource lock acquisition timed out. Indicates extreme resource contention or deadlocks in the storage engine.

rules.label: 🚨 PRIMARY STEPDOWN
rules.description: Operation was interrupted because the primary node stepped down to a secondary state. Check for cluster elections or maintenance.

rules.label: 🚨 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR
rules.description: A generic internal server error occurred. Check the mongod logs for stack traces or specific crash information.

rules.label: 🚨 TRANSACTION TIMEOUT
rules.description: The transaction or operation exceeded the allotted time limit (maxTimeMS or transaction lifetime). Verify timeout settings.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Excessive Dirty Cache (>100 MB)
rules.description: The transaction dirtied a massive amount of WiredTiger cache memory. This can severely degrade global cluster performance by forcing aggressive evictions.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Cache Pressure Stall ({value_duration})
rules.description: WiredTiger cache pressure detected. The operation was stalled while waiting for dirty data to be evicted to disk, indicating that the cache is full and I/O cannot keep up with write volume.

rules.label: 🚨 [TRACE] Index Amplification
rules.description: Massive index amplification detected. A single document mutation generated over 1,000 index keys, usually indicating an uncontrolled Multikey (Array) Index.
